memoryArtifacts.headline: Portrait study by Hans Holbein the Younger, c. 1533. The portrait was previously thought to be that of his cousin, Thomas Boleyn, but it has been officially identified as the 9th Earl of Ormond.

popularStory.headline: James death details. Found at St. Candice Cathedral, Kilkenny, Ireland
popularStory.description: He was poisoned in London and there buried. His heart was sent to Ireland and deposited in the St. Candice Cathedral in Kilkenny, Ireland.

zh.lifeSummary: 當James Butler於1496年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克金薩爾時，他的父親Sir Piers Butler29歲，母親Lady Margaret FitzGerald24歲。他於1532年與Lady Joan Fitzgerald結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1545年登記服兵役。他於1532年被列為he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]。他去世於1546年10月28日在英國米德萊可霍爾波恩享年50歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 基爾堅尼基爾堅尼。

nameMeanings.name: Butler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a word that originally denoted a wine steward, usually the chief servant of a medieval household, from Norman French butuiller (Old French bouteillier, Latin buticularius, from buticula ‘bottle’). In the large households of royalty and the most powerful nobility, the title came to denote an officer of high rank and responsibility, only nominally concerned with the supply of wine, if at all. As well as being widespread in England, this is also the surname of an important Irish family, descended from Theobald FitzWalter, who was appointed Chief Butler of Ireland by King Henry II in 1177. It is Gaelicized as de Buitléir.

English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English boteler ‘maker of bottles (usually of leather)’, a derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bo(u)teille ‘bottle’ and synonymous with Botelmaker.
Americanized form of French   Bouthillier   (see   Bouteiller  ).

variants.name: Buttler
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Butler  .
German: occupational name for a village tavern owner, from French bouteillier ‘butler’.
Americanized form of German Buttlar: habitational name from a place so named in Thuringia.

variants.name: Bouthillier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Jacques, Normand, Philippe, Yves.
French: variant of   Bouteiller  . This name is sometimes Americanized as   Butler  . Compare   Boutilier  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Butler was born in 1496, in Kinsale, County Cork, Ireland, his father, Sir Piers Butler, was 29 and his mother, Lady Margaret FitzGerald, was 24. He married Lady Joan Fitzgerald in 1532. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1545. In 1532, his occupation is listed as he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]. He died on 28 October 1546, in Holborn, Middlesex, England, at the age of 50, and was buried in Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Butler est né en 1496, à Kinsale, County Cork, Irlande, son père, Sir Piers Butler, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret FitzGerald, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Lady Joan Fitzgerald en 1532. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1545. En 1532, sa profession de son indiquée est he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]. Il est décédé le 28 octobre 1546, à Holborn, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlande.

Er hat 1532 Lady Joan Fitzgerald geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1545 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1532 ist sein Beruf als he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland] aufgeführt. Er starb am 28. Oktober 1546 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Holborn, Middlesex, England und wurde in Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irland begraben.

Lui sposato Lady Joan Fitzgerald nel 1532. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1545. Nel 1532, la sua occupazione è indicata come he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]. Morì il 28 ottobre 1546, nel Holborn, Middlesex, England, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.

ko.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리
ko.lifeSummary: James Butler(이)가 1496년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 킨세일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Piers Butler(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret FitzGerald(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1532년 Lady Joan Fitzgerald(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1545년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1532년, 그의 직업은 he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]이었다. 그는 1546년 10월 28일 영국 미들레세스 홀본에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 킬켄이 킬켄이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Butler nasceu em 1496, em Kinsale, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, Sir Piers Butler, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret FitzGerald, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Joan Fitzgerald em 1532. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1545. Em 1532, sua profissão aparece como he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]. Ele faleceu em 28 de outubro de 1546, em Holborn, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.

ru.County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irelan: коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Butler родился в 1496 г. в местности кинсеил, коунти корк, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Sir Piers Butler было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret FitzGerald, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Joan Fitzgerald в 1532 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. В 1545 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1532 годуработал по профессии he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]. Он умер 28 октября 1546 г. в местности холборн, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности килкенни, коунти килкенни, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Butler nació en 1496, en Kinsale, County Cork, Irlanda, su papá, Sir Piers Butler, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret FitzGerald, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Lady Joan Fitzgerald en 1532. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1545. En 1532, su ocupación registrada era he held the office of lord high treasurer [ireland]. Murió el 28 de octubre de 1546, en Holborn, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.
